name: Strong equivalence principle (Nordtvedt effect)
requirement: Any candidate framework must respect the strong equivalence principle - that the gravitational binding energy of a body falls in an external gravitational field at the same rate as the rest of its mass-energy. This is tested by lunar laser ranging (Earth-Moon-Sun system, where Earth has ~5e-10 fractional gravitational self-energy) and by the triple system PSR J0337+1715 (a millisecond pulsar in a hierarchical triple with two white dwarfs).

regime.description: Earth-Moon scale and stellar-binary scale; SEP test sensitivity grows with the gravitational self-energy of the test body.

current_bound.value: Delta_acc = (+0.5 +/- 1.8) x 10^-6 (PSR J0337+1715, Voisin 2020), implying |eta_N| < ~2 x 10^-5 after dividing by Delta(E_grav/Mc^2) ~ 0.15 between the neutron star and inner white dwarf; Delta_acc / Earth-Moon = (-0.8 +/- 1.3) x 10^-13 from LLR (Williams 2012), implying |eta_N| < 4.4 x 10^-4 after dividing by Delta(E_grav/Mc^2) ~ 4.4 x 10^-10
current_bound.units: Delta_acc = fractional acceleration difference (dimensionless); eta_N = Nordtvedt parameter (dimensionless)
current_bound.experiment: Lunar Laser Ranging + millisecond pulsar PSR J0337+1715
current_bound.reference: Voisin et al., 'An improved test of the strong equivalence principle with the pulsar in a triple star system', A&A 638, A24 (2020), arXiv:2005.01388; Archibald et al., Nature 559, 73 (2018), arXiv:1807.02059; Williams, Turyshev, Boggs, CQG 29, 184004 (2012), arXiv:1203.2150

what_it_excludes: Many alternative theories of gravity (Brans-Dicke-style scalar-tensor with low Brans-Dicke parameter omega, f(R) without screening) predict eta_N > 0 at observable levels. The PSR J0337+1715 bound is six orders of magnitude tighter than LLR for gravitational self-energy ratios characteristic of neutron stars.

parameterization.name: Nordtvedt parameter eta_N. In PPN: eta_N = 4 beta - gamma - 3 - (10/3) xi - alpha_1 + (2/3) alpha_2 - (2/3) zeta_1 - (1/3) zeta_2.
parameterization.summary: SEP violation makes a body's gravitational mass m_g differ from its inertial mass m_i by a factor depending on its gravitational self-energy: m_g / m_i = 1 + eta_N (E_grav / m_i c^2).

procedural.predicted_observable: Differential acceleration of a self-gravitating body in an external field, proportional to eta_N times its fractional gravitational self-energy.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: |eta_N| < ~2 x 10^-5 (PSR J0337+1715 via Voisin 2020 Delta_acc bound, dominant for strong-field SEP); |eta_N| < 4.4 x 10^-4 (LLR, weak-field).
framework_inputs: If the framework predicts SEP violation, compute eta_N either from the PPN parameter combination above, or directly from the dependence of the framework's gravitational self-energy contribution on the external potential.

primary_references.citation: Archibald et al., 'Universality of free fall from the orbital motion of a pulsar in a stellar triple system', Nature 559, 73 (2018)
Williams, Turyshev, Boggs, 'Lunar laser ranging tests of the equivalence principle', CQG 29, 184004 (2012)
Voisin et al., 'An improved test of the strong equivalence principle with the pulsar in a triple star system', A&A 638, A24 (2020)
